hops.context: Swing-up control of a double pendulum is a very classical problem in nonlinear control
there is also a different class of pendulum systems for which swing-up control is necessary: fully actuated systems
there is also a different class of pendulum systems for which swing-up control is necessary: fully actuated systems

question: Which type of system classification correctly describes the nature of double pendulums when considering their control mechanisms according to the swing-up control strategy?

options.A: Fully actuated systems, as they require precise control over all their movements and positions.
options.B: Underactuated systems, lacking sufficient actuators to control each degree of freedom directly.
options.C: Overactuated systems, where there are more control inputs than necessary for basic operation.
options.D: Partially actuated systems, since some parts of the pendulum are not directly controlled.

explanation: The correct answer is Fully Actuated Systems. According to the knowledge path and context, the double pendulum, when controlled by swing-up control, is classified as part of Fully Actuated Systems. This classification implies precise control over all movements and positions, different from underactuated systems which have more degrees of freedom than actuators.
